items.question: "I'll see you tomorrow." (reported a week later) → He said he'd see me ___.
explanations: 'Tomorrow' points to the wrong day now.

items.question: "I saw her yesterday." → She said she'd seen her ___.
explanations: 'Yesterday' no longer points to the right day.
'The next day' means the day after, changing the meaning.

items.question: "I like your jacket." (said to me) → He said he liked ___ jacket.
explanations: 'Your' still points to the wrong person.
'His' would mean a third person's jacket.

items.question: "We're leaving now." → They said they were leaving ___.
explanations: 'Now' refers to the wrong moment when reported later.
'Here' is a place word, not a time word.

items.question: "I love it here." → She said she loved it ___.
explanations: 'Here' points to the reporter's place, not the original.
'That' is a demonstrative, not a place word.

items.question: "I made these myself." → He said he'd made ___ himself.
explanations: 'These' points to things near the reporter.

items.question: "I'll finish this today." (reported the same day) → He said he'd finish it ___.
explanations: Correct! Reporting on the same day, 'today' can stay.
'The day before' changes the meaning to the past.
'The next day' points to the future.

items.question: "We visited them last week." → They said they'd visited them ___.
explanations: Correct! 'Last week' shifts to 'the week before'.
'Last week' no longer points to the right time.
'The next week' points forward, changing the meaning.

items.question: Correct this: 'She said she liked this restaurant here.'
options: She said she liked that restaurant there.
She said she liked this restaurant here.
She said she liked that restaurant here.
She said she liked this restaurant there.
